data.title: Semiconductor and AI software stocks see red amid tech stock selloff
data.description:
A number of semiconductor and AI-related software stocks were dropping during Thursday trading amid a broader sell-off in the technology sector.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) had declined 2.9%, which, if it holds, would be the largest single-day decline since March 12, when it fell 3.5%. 

Among semiconductor stocks, Nvidia (NVDA) was down 2.4%, AMD (AMD) had dropped 5.5%, and Intel (INTC) had declined 4.5%. Broadcom had declined 2%, Marvell (MRVL) had inched down 1%, and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) had fallen 4.5%.

Memory and storage companies continued their recent declines. Micron Technology (MU) had fallen 4.6% and is now down 14% over the past five trading days. Western Digital (WDC) had dipped 4.7%, Sandisk (SNDK) had plunged 8%, and Seagate Technology (STX) had dropped by 5%. However, all these stocks have experienced extraordinary gains over the past year, increasing by 293%, 560%, 1,000%, and 342%, respectively. 

Among software names, AppLovin (APP) demonstrated the largest decline on Thursday as it was down 9% by noon trading. The stock has now dropped 41% year-to-date. Palantir (PLTR) was down 3.7%. 

In contrast, multiple enterprise software stocks were in the green on Thursday. ServiceNow (NOW) had inched up 1.2%, Accenture (ACN) had gained 1.7%, Salesforce (CRM) had increased 2%, and Atlassian (TEAM) was up 2.7%

Leading enterprise AI and cloud infrastructure providers were trending down. Meta (META) had declined 7%, Amazon (AMZN) had slipped 0.7%, Microsoft (MSFT) was down 0.8%, and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) had dipped 2%.

The bulk of Wall Street edged lower on Thursday as investors kept a close eye on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions after Tehran dismissed a U.S. proposal aimed at ending the conflict. The S&P 500 Index (SP500) and the NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) were both down more than 1%.

data.title: Fundrise Innovation Fund sinks after Citron short call
data.description:
• Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) plunged 44% after a short report from Citron Research.
  • Citron disclosed that it's short Fundrise Innovation (VCX).
  • Fundrise (VCX) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. 
  • Shares of Fundrise (VCX) had risen ~400% since the closed-end fund listed last week.

data.title: Dixie Group outlines $13M profit improvement plan for 2026 amid industry headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Dixie Group, Inc. (DXYN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Daniel Frierson, Chairman & CEO, opened by reporting net sales of $63,487,000 for the fourth quarter of 2025, down 1.4% year-over-year, with a net loss of $3 million compared to a net loss of $7,198,000 in Q4 2024. For the full year 2025, net sales were $257,429,000, down 2.9% from 2024, and the net loss was $7,615,000, improved from $13 million in 2024. Frierson stated, "we continue to gain market share in the corporate market during this difficult period." He emphasized ongoing cost reductions and a new profit improvement plan for 2026 targeting over $13 million in enhanced profitability. Frierson also noted, "we reduced cost by over $12 million and now have reduced costs by $60 million over the last 3 years."
  • Allen Danzey, VP & CFO, highlighted, "we did generate higher gross margin at 27% on the quarter and the year -- full year of 2025 also at 27%. That compares to 21.7% in the prior year quarter and 24.7% than the prior fiscal year. These improved margins in 2025 were the result of cost reductions and profit improvement initiatives that we implemented throughout the year."

Outlook

  • Management is implementing a profit improvement plan for 2026 aiming to improve profitability by over $13 million. Frierson commented, "with the profit improvement plan in place and the introduction of many new products, 2026 will show significant improvement in our results even if market conditions do not improve." Danzey confirmed, "we're looking at $2.5 million as part of our plan for 2026 for the CapEx."
  • The company expects continued market share gains in a contracting industry and has adapted to high tariffs by raising prices three times during the year. However, Frierson cautioned about the impact of the Iranian situation on raw material costs and industry pricing going forward.

Financial Results

  • Gross margin for both Q4 and the full year 2025 was 27%. Selling and administrative expenses for 2025 decreased by 3.1% from the prior year, with legal settlements driving $1.2 million in other operating expenses.
  • Interest expense increased to $7.3 million for the year, up from $6.4 million in 2024, attributed to higher internal interest rates and financing fees. Receivables and net inventory were slightly lower year-over-year. Accounts payable and accrued expenses rose to $38.8 million from $30 million due to extended payment terms.
  • Debt balance, net of cash, decreased by $7.6 million from the previous year-end. Capital expenditures were approximately $600,000, and borrowing availability under the senior credit facility is estimated at $10 million, subject to a $6 million excess availability requirement.

Q&A

  • Barry Blank, J.H. Darbie & Co., Inc.: Asked about the impact of potential interest rate increases on the business. Frierson responded, "I believe the level of existing home sales has been pretty steady at the $4 million range for the last couple of years when rates were higher. So I don't anticipate they'll go down a lot more, but we clearly will not have improved volumes, and we're not projecting improved volumes for this year."
  • Blank inquired about the effect of the Iranian situation on materials. Frierson replied, "I'm not as concerned about getting the material as the price of the material... the bigger impact will likely be rising raw material costs and passing those on in an expeditious manner."
  • Blank suggested management stock purchases and a stock dividend to restore confidence. Frierson said, "I don't disagree with you. And we will be looking at all of your thoughts there with our Board at our next Board meeting."
  • Michael Hughes, SGF Capital Management, LP: Sought clarification on the $14.2 million in cost saves for 2026. Danzey and Frierson confirmed this is year-over-year incremental improvement. Hughes also asked about asset sales, to which Danzey replied, "We have buildings in North Georgia as well as equipment that we could take out for financing. We have looked at these opportunities. We'll continue to pursue those."
  • Hughes asked about the $1.4 million net tariff impact timing, and Frierson confirmed the bulk was in the third and fourth quarters.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on cost savings, asset sales, and strategies to address the depressed stock price, showing a slightly negative tone, especially regarding external macro risks and management alignment with shareholders. Blank's comments on the record-low stock price signaled concern.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, focusing on cost control, profit improvement, and market share gains. Statements such as "we are hopeful that this will happen in the not-too-distant future" and "with the profit improvement plan in place... 2026 will show significant improvement" indicate a blend of hope and confidence, yet with open acknowledgment of industry volatility.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remains focused on operational discipline and future improvement, while analysts expressed more pointed concerns about external shocks and tangible actions to support the share price.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 now includes a $13 million profit improvement plan, up from a $10 million plan mentioned the previous quarter. The company has raised prices multiple times to offset tariffs, compared to fewer increases previously.
  • Strategic focus has shifted from primarily cost controls and legal settlements in Q3 to now emphasizing operational restructuring, continued market share gains, and navigating new geopolitical risks.
  • Analysts in Q4 raised more questions about management actions to support the stock, while in Q3 their focus was on legal settlements, liquidity, and the impact of price increases.
  • Key metrics, including gross margin and net loss, improved sequentially, and the company continues to highlight reduced expenses and lower debt.
  • Management's confidence in restructuring and cost initiatives persists, but there is more explicit caution about macroeconomic and geopolitical risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Frierson cited the Iranian situation as a major uncertainty, with potential for "a major impact on input costs for our industry" if hostilities persist.
  • Tariffs and the timing of price increases had a negative $1.4 million impact, with $3.3 million in total IEPPA tariff payments for the year. The company is actively seeking refunds for these tariffs.
  • Higher interest expense and extended payment terms on payables were noted.
  • Ongoing risk from weak housing turnover and potential for further increases in raw material costs are key concerns.

Final Takeaway

The Dixie Group is navigating persistent industry decline and macroeconomic uncertainty with an expanded cost reduction and profit improvement plan targeting over $13 million in 2026. Management highlights ongoing market share gains and operational streamlining, while acknowledging external risks such as tariffs and geopolitical disruptions. The company anticipates significant improvement in results even if market conditions remain challenging, underpinned by new product introductions, strategic cost actions, and a focus on premium market segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Top Quant rated undervalued mid-cap stocks with high-growth grades
data.description:
Amid ongoing market volatility fueled by geopolitical tensions, below is a list for mid‑cap investors seeking stability in undervalued growth plays. The list contains top performers on valuation and growth metrics, ranked by Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings.

These names were specifically filtered for their standout A grades in those key factors, offering potential bargains even as broader indexes whipsaw.

United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) tops the list with a near-perfect Quant Rating of 4.96, earning both an A+ valuation grade and an A+ growth grade. The Hanover Insurance Group, Inc. (THG), Galaxy Digital Inc. (GLXY), and Plains GP Holdings, L.P. (PAGP) follow closely behind, each with ratings above 4.65. Notably, the first six stocks on the list all carry a “Strong Buy” qualitative rating, spanning diverse sectors from food distribution to financial technology with Sezzle Inc. (SEZL) and real estate with EPR Properties (EPR).

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI), Quant Rating: 4.96

  2. The Hanover Insurance Group, Inc. (THG), Quant Rating: 4.75

  3. Galaxy Digital Inc. (GLXY), Quant Rating: 4.67

  4. Plains GP Holdings, L.P. (PAGP), Quant Rating: 4.66

  5. EPR Properties (EPR), Quant Rating: 4.62

  6. Sezzle Inc. (SEZL), Quant Rating: 4.56

  7. StandardAero, Inc. (SARO), Quant Rating: 3.84

  8. SentinelOne, Inc. (S), Quant Rating: 3.76

  9. American Airlines Group Inc. (AAL), Quant Rating: 3.34

  10. HNI Corporation (HNI), Quant Rating: 3.29

 Mid-Cap ETFs: (IJH), (VO), (IWR), (MDY), (VOE), (VOT), (SCHM), (IJK), and (IJJ)

data.title: Stryker rebounds as most manufacturing activity restored after cyberattack
data.description:
• Stryker (SYK) shares ended a seven-day decline on Thursday after Bloomberg reported that the U.S. MedTech firm has restored most of its manufacturing operations following a cyberattack that disrupted its global systems earlier this month.
  • “Manufacturing capability is quickly ramping with most of our sites and critical lines restored." Stryker (SYK) said in an emailed statement.
  • Shares of the surgical equipment maker fell sharply on Mar. 11 after news of the cyberattack for which Handala, a pro-Palestinian hacking group, claimed responsibility.
  • The incident disrupted its information systems and business applications, running on Microsoft’s (MSFT) Windows operating systems, impacting order processing, manufacturing, and shipping.
  • Healthcare providers were forced to delay surgical procedures involving Stryker (SYK) products as the company struggled to fulfill orders.
  • Michigan-based Stryker (SYK), with a nearly 56,000-strong global workforce, is a leading manufacturer of surgical robots, orthopedic equipment, and implants.

data.title: Mortgage rates continue to rise, but housing market is gradually improving
data.description:
Mortgage rates continued to increase this week, but the housing market is seen to be gradually improving, the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.38% as of March 26, up from 6.22% last week but below 6.65% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.75%, up from 5.54% last week and down from 5.89% a year ago.

"The housing market continues to show gradual improvements compared to a year ago amid recent rate volatility. Purchase and refinance applications are up year-over-year, and rates remain lower than last year," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: M-tron signals expanded acquisition strategy and $76.4M backlog amid strong defense demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: M-tron Industries, Inc. (MPTI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Cameron Pforr, CFO & CEO, highlighted a strong finish to fiscal 2025, emphasizing "continued strength in M-tron sales and good financial performance for Q4 FY 2025." He noted, "Bookings growth was particularly strong with a 62% increase in backlog over the 2024 year-end figure."
  • Pforr stated, "Our revenues continue to be driven by defense-related orders and strong improvement in the commercial aircraft or what we call the avionics sector."
  • The company made "strategic investments in research and development and continued our efforts to increase the market profile of the company."
  • Pforr referenced the recent signing of 7-year production agreements between Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, and the Department of War, with M-tron as a significant vendor to both companies' precision-guided munition programs, calling this "one of the most significant growth areas for M-tron."
  • On capital structure, Pforr stated, "We added $10.7 million of cash through operations. In December 2025, we sent a new loan agreement with Fifth Third Bank...Currently, we have no debt outstanding and haven't drawn upon this line."
  • The company closed a warrant offering, raising $27.5 million in net proceeds for potential acquisitions. Pforr said, "This offering is designed to support accretive acquisitions, the ability to perform carve-outs or participate in carve-outs, transactions of scale, the pursuit of strategic investments and also to expand our internal capabilities to meet demand."
  • Pforr announced an upcoming rights offering to further expand acquisition flexibility, with details on share pricing to be disclosed next week.
  • Pforr noted, "At the end of the period, M-tron had $20.9 million of cash on the balance sheet, with an additional $27.5 million being transferred early January 2026 to the company from our transfer agent as warrant proceeds."

Outlook

  • Management expects continued expansion of the defense program business, particularly in radar and electronic warfare, stating, "Two of the areas where we expect strong growth in '26 and '27 are radar and electronic warfare."
  • Pforr identified "strong growth throughout the year from commercial avionic shipments as we serve key suppliers to both Boeing and Airbus."
  • The rights offering is expected to launch in the coming week, with the subscription price anticipated at a 10% to 12% discount to the trailing 5-day volume weighted average price per share.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for Q4 2025 were $14.2 million, an 11.2% increase over the same period last year, driven by "strong defense program product as well as avionics shipments."
  • Gross margins for Q4 2025 were 46.9% compared to 47.2% in Q4 2024, impacted by tariffs and product mix.
  • Net income per diluted share was $0.99 for Q4 2025, compared to $0.73 in the prior year's period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $4.5 million for Q4 2025, a 46.8% increase over the previous year's quarter.
  • Backlog at quarter-end was $76.4 million, up from $47.2 million at the end of 2024.
  • For fiscal 2025, total revenues were $54.4 million, with gross margins at 44.4% and net income at $8.4 million, or $2.62 per diluted share.
  • The company reversed $860,000 in previously accrued incentive compensation expense, positively impacting Q4 results by 6%.

Q&A

  • Anja Soderstrom, Sidoti & Company, LLC: "What's the motivation for this rights offering?" Cameron Pforr: "It's really more being driven by some of the pressure from the industry, from the Department of War on primes to become more nimble...having a stronger balance sheet is going to allow M-tron to take advantage of the opportunities."
  • Anja Soderstrom: "How should we think about gross margin for next year?" Pforr: "We do expect a slightly more favorable tariff environment next year...In 2025, tariffs impacted our gross margins by about 1%. We do expect them to be a little bit less in '26 and forward."
  • Anja Soderstrom: "What was most of the growth [in backlog] coming from?" Pforr: "The backlog was driven by defense and aerospace and then avionics. Both of those were strong developer growth areas in the backlog."
  • Unknown Analyst: "How meaningful is the drone exposure today in your business? And can it become a more material part of the mix over time?" Pforr: "We do sell solutions and components to several of the manufacturers of drones...In the prior year, we typically do like $1.5 million to $2 million of revenue in that space. But I think that's going to grow."
  • Unknown Analyst: "Can you quantify the radar systems revenue for the 2026?" Pforr: "I think it will probably total around roughly $4 million probably."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the company’s acquisition strategy, gross margin outlook, and backlog growth, maintaining a neutral tone with probing questions about forward plans and market exposure.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly optimistic tone in prepared remarks, with Pforr stating, "We are well positioned to continue to perform well with a significant upside potential given Department of War's actions and the recent conflicts in Iran." During Q&A, Pforr was measured but direct, acknowledging uncertainty in tariffs and product mix.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remains probing but less focused on operational risks, while management appears more confident due to the strengthened balance sheet and expanded backlog.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize larger acquisition opportunities and flexibility through additional capital-raising, compared to the prior quarter's focus on R&D and operational execution.
  • Strategic focus has expanded, with management now highlighting inorganic growth and strategic partnerships alongside organic growth.
  • Key metrics improved: Q4 revenue and EBITDA both increased, and backlog grew substantially from $58.8 million at Q3 end to $76.4 million at Q4 end.
  • Management tone has shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to a more assertive and confident stance in Q4, supported by financial results and market opportunities.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from operational details and partnerships in Q3 to capital allocation and long-term demand visibility in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified tariffs and product mix as ongoing challenges impacting gross margins, with Pforr noting, "We do expect a slightly more favorable tariff environment next year...it's a moving target."
  • The company acknowledged the impact of new product introductions on margins and referenced uncertainty in timing for increased production related to new defense contracts.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of margin improvement and the materiality of revenue from drone and radar systems.

Final Takeaway

M-tron's Q4 2025 call outlined a company leveraging strong defense sector demand to expand its backlog 62% year-over-year, while proactively raising capital for acquisitions through a rights offering and warrant proceeds. Management emphasized both organic and inorganic growth strategies, highlighted by upcoming opportunities in radar, electronic warfare, and commercial avionics, and pointed to a strengthened balance sheet as a foundation for future expansion. The company faces ongoing challenges from tariffs and evolving product mix, but remains confident in its ability to capitalize on new defense contracts and strategic market trends.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Brown-Forman jumps on report Pernod Ricard weighing purchase
data.description:
Brown-Forman (BF.B) soared 11% on a report that Pernod Ricard SA (PDRDF) is weighing a potential purchase of the maker of Jack Daniel's whiskey.

Pernod Ricard (PDRDF) has had some initial talks with Brown-Forman (BF.B) about a deal, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Deliberations are ongoing, and there’s no certainty they will result in a deal, the people told Bloomberg. 

Pernod Ricard and Brown-Forman didn’t immediately respond to Bloomberg requests for comment.

data.title: Hertz and Avis rally as airport gridlock fuels demand for car rentals
data.description:
Airport security delays during the partial government shutdown and subsequent shortage of Transportation Security Administration agents are giving a nice boost to the car rental business as travelers ditch air travel for road trips.  

According to data compiled by Hertz (HTZ) reported by TravelPulse, search traffic to the car rental’s website has increased ~15%, with results indicating 33% of rentals are for road trips and driving vacations. The trend mirrors a comparable spike in car rentals during the last government shutdown in November.

To help travelers avoid the airport snarls, Hertz (HTZ) is recommending travelers book at neighborhoods, prepay for fuel to lock in prices, and add flight information if necessary to ensure availability in the event of long airport delays and missed flights. 

Hertz (HTZ) is also offering 25% off last-minute reservations and one-way rentals at airports.

The spike in demand for rental cars is driving up shares of Hertz (HTZ) and Avis Budget Group (CAR), both adding double-digit gains on Thursday to a four-day advance of 26% and 40%, respectively.

data.title: Semiconductor equipment stocks may 'peak' in late 2026 based on past cycles: BNP
data.description:
Semiconductor equipment stocks may start to “peak” in the latter part of 2026, if history is any guidance, investment firm BNP Paribas said on Thursday. 

“Semicap stocks have rallied 2x in six months, with the share price rally closely correlated with the increase in memory prices,” analyst David O’Connor wrote in a note to clients. “64% of the share price increase has stemmed from multiple expansion (now at record levels) and only 27% from cons. EPS revisions. While there is much debate on the sustainability of this rally, we remain in the camp that believes memory continues to be a commodity and a cost-plus business. We analyze our Semicap names through the lens of previous cycles and flag the risk to our stocks in a correction scenario (c. 50%). With stocks trading at record multiples and discounting mid- to high-teens WFE growth in 2026-27, it's hard to see what drives stocks higher.”

O'Connor has a Neutral rating on Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), and KLA Corp. (KLAC).

Delving deeper, O'Connor said the previous cycles have seen share price peaks come three quarters after the multiples peaked. And with that, revenues peaked one quarter after that, and then memory prices corrected around the same time “meaningful” additional supply became available.

“In the last two corrections (Cloud cycle 2017, COVID 2021), memory prices fell an average of 55% and Semicap share prices fell 43%, primarily driven by multiple compression. Implication for current cycle: implies share price peak in late 2026 or early 2027 With meaningful memory supply coming online in H2'27, overlaying the above Semicap cycle behavior on the current cycle suggests a memory price correction in H2'27. This would be preceded by a Semicap rev. peak in mid-2027, with share price peaking in late 2026/early 2027 and PE multiple peaking in Q2'26. We note the PE multiple appears to have already peaked for Semicap [companies].”
